title: Implement wave runner with parallel agent spawning
description:
Create the core wave execution function that spawns parallel Claude agents for all tasks in a wave and collects results.

Implementation:
- runWave(ctx, wave, workDir) []TaskResult
- Spawns one goroutine per task in the wave
- Each goroutine calls agent.Run() with WorkDir set to worktree path
- Each agent gets a StateCallback that writes per-task .live.json via runrecord.WriteLive()
- Collects results (output, cost, tokens, signals) per task
- Respects context cancellation for graceful shutdown
- Parses signals from agent output (reuse existing ParseSignals from engine/signals.go)

This replaces both pool mode (mutex-based task claiming) and swarm mode (Claude-as-orchestrator).
acceptance_criteria: runWave() spawns parallel agents and returns per-task results. Board receives per-task live updates via .live.json files. Signal parsing works on wave results. Tests cover: single task wave, multi-task wave, context cancellation, signal detection.
closed_reason: Wave runner implemented with parallel agent spawning, signal parsing, and live record updates.
